children.text: The minimum age for obtaining a PPL is:

answers: 16 years
17 years
18 years
21 years
correctAnswer: 17 years

children.text: To be able to execute a public transport flight, the minimum and maximum age (with ATPL) is:

answers: 16 and 60 years
21 and 59 years
17 and 59 years
18 and 60 years
correctAnswer: 21 and 59 years

children.text: Which of the following Annexes to the Chicago convention contains minimum specifications for a crew licence to have international validity?

answers: Annex 2
Annex 1
Annex 3
Annex 4
correctAnswer: Annex 1

children.text: The holder of a pilot's licence should inform the Authority of any illness which they are suffering which involves incapacity to undertake those functions to which the licence relates throughout a period of a certain number of days or more. The number of days is:
If a licence holder is unable to perform the flight crew functions appropriate to that licence due to illness, the authority must be informed:

answers: if still not fit to fly when his/her current medical certificate expires
as soon as possible if the illness is expected to last more than 21days
After 21 days of consecutive "illness"
after one calendar month of consecutive illness
correctAnswer: After 21 days of consecutive "illness"

children.text: The age of an applicant for a commercial pilot licence shall not be less than:

answers: 17 years of age
18 years of age
16 years of age
21 years of age
correctAnswer: 18 years of age

children.text: An applicant for a commercial pilot licence shall hold

answers: a current class II medical assessment
a current class III medical assessment
a current class I medical assessment
a current class medical assessment as prescribed by the state issuing the licence
correctAnswer: a current class I medical assessment

children.text: The privileges of the holder of a commercial pilot licence-aeroplane shall be:

answers: none of the answers are correct
to act as pilot in command in any aeroplane engaged in commercial air transportation
to act as pilot in command in any aeroplane certificate for single pilot operation other than in commercial air transportation
to act as pilot in command in any aeroplane engaged in operations other than commercial air transportation
correctAnswer: to act as pilot in command in any aeroplane engaged in operations other than commercial air transportation

children.text: An applicant for a commercial pilot licence-aeroplane shall have completed not less than ... hours of cross country flight time as pilot in command including a cross country flight totalling not less than ... km (-NM), in the course of which full stop landings at two different aerodromes shall be made. The hours and distance refered are:

answers: 20 hours and 540 km (300NM)
15 hours and 540 km (300NM)
10 hours and 270 km (150 NM)
20 hours and 270 km (150NM)
correctAnswer: 20 hours and 540 km (300NM)

children.text: An applicant for a commercial pilot licence aeroplane shall have completed in aeroplanes not less than:

answers: 10 hours of cross country flight time as pilot-in-command including a cross country flight not less than 540 km (300NM)
20 hours of cross country flight time as pilot-in-command including a cross country flight not less than 540 km (300NM)
15 hours of cross country flight time as pilot-in-command including a cross country flight not less than 540 km (300NM)
25 hours of cross country flight time as pilot-in-command including a cross country flight not less than 540 km (300NM)
correctAnswer: 20 hours of cross country flight time as pilot-in-command including a cross country flight not less than 540 km (300NM)

children.text: An applicant holding a private or commercial pilot licence aeroplane for the issue of an instrument rating, shall have completed ... hours of cross-country flight time as pilot-in-command of aircraft in categories acceptable to the licensing Authority, of which not less than ..... hours shall be in aeroplanes. The said hours, are respectively

answers: 50 hours and 15 hours
40 hours and 10 hours
50 hours and 10 hours
40 hours and 15 hours
correctAnswer: 50 hours and 10 hours

children.text: In certain circumstances a medical examination may be deferred at the discretion of the licensing authority, provided that such deferment shall only be made as an exception and shall not exceed:

answers: in the case of a private pilot, a single period of 12 months
Two consecutive periods each of three months in the case of a flight crew member of an aircraft engaged in non commercial operations
A single period of six months in the case of a flight crew member of an aircraft engaged in commercial operations
A single period of six months in the case of a flight crew member of an aircraft engaged in non commercial operations
correctAnswer: A single period of six months in the case of a flight crew member of an aircraft engaged in non commercial operations

children.text: The duration of the period of currency of a medical assessment shall begin on the date:

answers: the licence is delivered to the pilot
the licence is issued or renewed
the medical assessment is issued
the licence is issued or validated
correctAnswer: the medical assessment is issued

children.text: When a contracting state renders valid a licence issued by another contracting state the validity of the authorization

answers: shall not extend more than 15 days from the date of the licence
the Contracting state rendering a licence valid may extend the date of the validity at its own discretion
shall not extend beyond the period of validity of the licence
shall not extend beyond the period of validity of the licence other than for use in private flights
correctAnswer: shall not extend beyond the period of validity of the licence

children.text: Type ratings shall be established

answers: for any type of aircraft whenever considered necessary by the authority
all the answers are correct
only aircraft certificated for operation with a minimum crew of at least two pilots
only for aircraft certificated for operation with a minimum crew of at least two pilots and each type of helicopter
correctAnswer: for any type of aircraft whenever considered necessary by the authority

children.text: For commercial pilot licence aeroplane the applicant shall have completed in aeroplanes not less than if the privileges of the licence are to be exercised at night

answers: 5 hours of night flight time including 5 take offs and 5 landings as pilot in command
5 hours of night flight time including 3 take-offs and 3 landings as pilot in command
5 hours of night flight time including 5 take offs and 5 landings either as pilot in command or as co-pilot
5 hours of night flight time including 3 take offs and 5 landings as pilot in command
correctAnswer: 5 hours of night flight time including 5 take offs and 5 landings as pilot in command

children.text: An applicant for a commercial pilot licence aeroplane shall have completed in aeroplanes not less than:

answers: 200 hours of flight time and 70 hours as pilot in command
200 hours of flight time or 150 hours if completed during a course of approved training as a pilot of aeroplanes
200 hours of flight time and 80 hours as pilot in command
150 hours of flight time and 100 hours as pilot in command
correctAnswer: 200 hours of flight time or 150 hours if completed during a course of approved training as a pilot of aeroplanes

children.text: An applicant for a commercial pilot licence shall have completed in aeroplanes not less than:

answers: 10 hours of instrument instruction time of which not more than 5 hours may be instrument ground time
20 hours of instrument instruction time of which not more than 10 hours may be instrument ground time
20 hours of instrument instruction time of which not more than 5 hours may be instrument ground time
15 hours of instrument time of which not more than 5 hours as pilot in command
correctAnswer: 10 hours of instrument instruction time of which not more than 5 hours may be instrument ground time

children.text: The International Civil Aviation Convention Annex containing standards and recommended practices for Personnel Licensing is:

answers: Annex 2
Annex 1
Annex 12
Annex 11
correctAnswer: Annex 1

children.text: The applicant to exercise the functions of an Instrumental Flight Rating in aeroplanes shall prove, according to Annex I, PERSONNEL LICENSING, his/her capability to pilot such aircraft only by instrumental rules and an engine

answers: Land/inactive
Multi-engine / inoperative or simulated inoperative
Amphibious/inactive or simulated inactive
Single-engine/inactive
correctAnswer: Multi-engine / inoperative or simulated inoperative

children.text: According to JAR-FCL, a professionnal flight crew licence license issued by a non JAA State may be rendered valid for use on aircraft registered in a JAA Member State

answers: At the discretion of the Authority of the Member State concerned for a period not exceeding the period validity of basic licence
At the discretion of the Authority of that Member State concerned for a period not exceeding one year, provided that the basic licence remains valid
At the discretion of the Authority of that Member State concerned for a period not exceeding one year
At the discretion of the Authority of that Member State concerned for a period not exceeding two years
correctAnswer: At the discretion of the Authority of that Member State concerned for a period not exceeding one year, provided that the basic licence remains valid

children.text: According to JAR-FCL, licence holders do not exercise the privileges of their licences, related ratings or authorisations at any time when they are aware of any decrease in their medical fitness  which might render them unable to safely exercise those privileges. They shall without undue delay seek the advice of the authority or AME when becoming aware of hospital or clinic admissions for:

answers: More than 12 hours
Any period
More than one week
More than 12 days
correctAnswer: More than 12 hours

children.text: According to JAR-FCL, Class 2 medical certificate for private pilots will be valid for

answers: 60 months until age of 30, 24 months until age of 40, 12 months thereafter
24 months until age of 40, 12 months thereafter
24 months until age of 40, 12 months until age of 60 and 6 months thereafter
60 months until age of 30, 24 months until age of 50, 12 months thereafter
correctAnswer: 60 months until age of 30, 24 months until age of 50, 12 months thereafter

children.text: According to JAR-FCL, an applicant for a CPL (A) who has satisfactorily followed and completed an integrated flying training course shall have completed as a pilot of aeroplanes having a certificate of airworthiness issued or accepted by a JAA Member State at least:

answers: 150 hours of flight time plus 10 hours of instrument ground time
200 hours of flight time plus 10 hours of instrument ground time
200 hours of flight time
150 hours of flight time
correctAnswer: 150 hours of flight time

children.text: According to JAR-FCL, an instrument rating is valid for:

answers: The period of validity of the licence
two years
one year
Indefinitely
correctAnswer: one year

children.text: According to JAR-FCL, an applicant for an IR(A) shall hold a PPL (A)including a night qualification or CPL(A) and shall have completed at least 50 hours:

answers: Instructional flight time as student-pilot-in-command of aeroplanes or helicopters of which at least 10 hours shall be in aeroplanes
Cross country flight time as pilot-in-command in aeroplanes or helicopters of which at least 10 hours shall be in aeroplanes
Cross country flight time as pilot of aeroplanes or helicopters of which at least 10 hours shall be in aeroplanes
Instructional flight time as studen-pilot-in-command of aeroplanes
correctAnswer: Cross country flight time as pilot-in-command in aeroplanes or helicopters of which at least 10 hours shall be in aeroplanes

children.text: According to JAR-FCL, class rating shall be established for single pilots aeroplanes not requiring a type rating, including:

answers: All self.-sustaining gliders
Any other type of aeroplane if considered necessary
All types of single-pilot, single-engine aeroplanes fitted with a turbojet engine
Microlights having fixed wings and moveable aerodynamic control surfaces acting in all three dimensions
correctAnswer: All self.-sustaining gliders

children.text: According to JAR-FCL, establishment of separate type rating for aeroplanes will be assessed on the basis of three criteria. One of these three criteria is that the aeroplane has:

answers: Handling characteristics that require additional flying or simulator training
Handling characteristics that require the use of more than one crew member
A certificate of airworthiness issued by a non-member state
A certificate of airworthiness issued by the manufacturer
correctAnswer: Handling characteristics that require additional flying or simulator training

children.text: According to JAR-FCL, the validity of type ratings and multi-engine class ratings will be one year from the date:

answers: Of the last medical certificate
Of the skill test
Of issue
The application is received by the Authority
correctAnswer: Of issue

children.text: According to JAR-FCL, single pilot single-engine class ratings are valid for:

answers: Two years
One year
Two years up to age 40 years then one year thereafter
Five years after licence issuie
correctAnswer: Two years

children.text: According to JAR-FCL, the privileges of a newly qualified Flight Instructor are restricted to carrying out instruction under the supervision of a FI(A), approved for this purpose. The restrictions may be removed from the rating:

answers: On the recommendation of the supervising FI(A)after the holder of the restricted FI(A) rating has supervised at least 100 solo flights
On the recommendation of the supervising FI(A) after the holder of the restricted FI(A) rating has completed a competency test
On the recommendation of the supervising FI(A) after the holder of the restricted FI(A) rating has supervised at least 100 solo flights and completed a competency test

On the recommendation of the supervising FI(A) after the holder of the restricted FI(A)rating has completed at least 100 hours flight instruction and , in addition, has supervised at least 25 student solo flights
correctAnswer: On the recommendation of the supervising FI(A) after the holder of the restricted FI(A)rating has completed at least 100 hours flight instruction and , in addition, has supervised at least 25 student solo flights

children.text: According to JAR-FCL, the aeroplane instructor categories recognised are:

answers: FI(A)/TRI(A)CRE(A)/IRE(A) and SFI authorisation
FI(A) and IRI(A)
FI(A)/TRI(A)/CRI(A)/IRI(A) and SFI authorisation
FE(A)/TRE(A)/CRE(A)/IRE(A) and SFI authorisation
correctAnswer: FI(A)/TRI(A)/CRI(A)/IRI(A) and SFI authorisation

children.text: According to JAR-FCL, the privileges of the holder of an unrestricted FI(A) rating are to conduct flight instruction for the issue of a CPL(A):

answers: Provided that the FI(A) has completed at least 500 hours of flight time as a pilot of aeroplanes including at least 200 hours of flight instruction
Provided that the FI(A) has completed not less than 15 hours on the relevant type in the preceding 12 months
Without restriction
Provided that the FI(A) has completed 200 hours of flight instruction
correctAnswer: Provided that the FI(A) has completed at least 500 hours of flight time as a pilot of aeroplanes including at least 200 hours of flight instruction

children.text: According to JAR-FCL, an examiner's authorisation is valid for:

answers: Not more than three years
The period of validity of the class/type rating
Not more than two years
The period of validity of the medical certificate
correctAnswer: Not more than three years

children.text: According to JAR-FCL, Medical certificates classes are:

answers: 1, 2 and 3
1 and 2
class 1 only
correctAnswer: 1 and 2

children.text: (IR) The validity period of an IR (A) is

answers: two years for single engine aeroplanes and one year for multi-engine aeroplane
one year
six month for multi-engine jet aeroplanes
two years for single-engine aeroplanes
correctAnswer: one year

children.text: (CPL) Which of the statements concerning the privileges of a holder of a CPL (A) is correct:

answers: act as PIC in commercial air transportation of any aircraft with a MTOW of 5700 kg or less
act as PIC in commercial air transportation
act as PIC in commercial air transportation on VFR flights only
act as PIC in commercial air transportation of any single-pilot aeroplane
correctAnswer: act as PIC in commercial air transportation of any single-pilot aeroplane

children.text: (CPL) The minimum age for a CPL (A) shall be at least:

answers: 17 years
18 years
19 years
20 years
correctAnswer: 18 years

children.text: (CPL) An applicant for a CPL (A) shall have completed at least the following flight time:

answers: 150 hours if following an integrated course
50 hours cross country flight as PIC in aeroplanes
150 hours if following a modular course
200 hours if following an integrated course
correctAnswer: 150 hours if following an integrated course

children.text: Except when a clearence is obtained from an ATC unit,a VFR flight can not enter or leave a control zone when ceiling is less than:

answers: 1 000 feet or visibility is less than 5 km
1 500 feet or visibility is less than 5 km
1 000 feet or visibility is less than 8 km
2 000 feet or visibility is less than 5 km
correctAnswer: 1 500 feet or visibility is less than 5 km

children.text: The person who has final authority as to the disposition of an aircraft during flight time is:

answers: The ATC controller if the aircraft is flying in a controlled airspace
The airliner operator
The aircraft owner
The commander
correctAnswer: The commander

children.text: Which of the following flights has the greatest priority to land?

answers: Emergency aircraft
Hospital aircraft carrying a very sick person needing immediate medical Attention
VIP (Head of state) aircraft
Military aircraft
correctAnswer: Emergency aircraft

children.text: An aircraft flying above the sea between 4 500 feet MSL and 9 000 feet MSL outside controlled airspace under VFR, must remain on principle at least:

answers: Clear of clouds and in sight of the surface; 8 km visibility
1 500 m horizontally, 1000 feet vertically from clouds; 5 km visibility
2 000 feet horizontally, 1000 feet vertically from clouds; 5 km visibility
1500 m horizontally, 1000 feet vertically from clouds; 8 km visibility
correctAnswer: 1 500 m horizontally, 1000 feet vertically from clouds; 5 km visibility

children.text: The VMC minima for an airspace classified as "G" above 10 000 feet MSL are:

answers: 1500 m horizontally and 1 000 feet vertically from clouds; 8 km visibility
1500 m horizontally and 1 000 feet verticaly from clouds; 5 km visibility
1 nautical mile horizontally and 1 000 feet vertically from clouds; 5 km visibility
1 nautical mile horizontally and 1000 feet vertically from clouds; 8 km visibility
correctAnswer: 1500 m horizontally and 1 000 feet vertically from clouds; 8 km visibility

children.text: A controlled flight is requested to inform the appropriate ATC unit whenever the average True Air Speed at cruising level varies or is expected to vary from that given in the flight plan by plus or minus:
An aircraft intercepted by another aircraft, if equipped with SSR transponder shall, unless  otherwise instructed by the appropriate ATS unit, select one of the following code on mode "A"
An aircraft intercepted by another aircraft shall immediately attempt to establish radio communication with the intercepting aircraft on the following frequencies:

answers: 121.5 MHz - 243 MHz
243 MHz - 125.5 MHz
correctAnswer: 121.5 MHz - 243 MHz

children.text: Which manoeuvre shall be executed by an intercepting aircraft if the pilot wants to communicate to the intercepted aircraft "YOU MAY PROCEED"?

answers: Executing a climbing turn of 90 degrees or more without crossing the line of flight of the intercepted aircraft
Circling the intercepted aircraft in a clock-wise pattern
Rocking the wings and flashing the navigational lights
Rocking wings twice and crossing in front of the aircraft
correctAnswer: Executing a climbing turn of 90 degrees or more without crossing the line of flight of the intercepted aircraft

children.text: Which action shall be taken by an aircraft in the traffic pattern of an aerodrome, experiencing radio failure to indicate difficulties which compel it to land without requiring immediate assistance?

answers: The repeated switching on and off of the landing lights
Switching on and off four times the landing lights
Switching on and off three times the landing lights
Switching on and off four times the navigation lights
correctAnswer: The repeated switching on and off of the landing lights

children.text: If radio communication is established during an interception but communications in a common language is not possible, which phrase should be pronounced by the intercepting aircraft to request the intercepted aircraft to descend for landing?

answers: Descend for landing
Descend
Let down
You land
correctAnswer: Descend

children.text: If radio contact with the intercepting aircraft is established but communication on a common language is not possible, which phrase should be pronounced by the intercepted aircraft to communicate that he is unable to comply with the instructions received?

answers: NOT POSSIBLE
UNABLE TO COMPLY
CAN NOT
CAN NOT COMPLY
correctAnswer: CAN NOT

children.text: A flashing red light from control tower during an approach to land means:

answers: Continue circling and wait for further instructions
The airport is unsafe, do not land
Give way to other aircraft in emergency
The airport is temporarily closed, continue circling
correctAnswer: The airport is unsafe, do not land

children.text: On aerodromes aircraft taxying on the manoeuvring area of an aerodrome shall give way to:

answers: other converging aircraft
other vehicles and pedestrians
all vehicles moving on the apron except the "follow me" vehicle
aircraft taking off or about to take off
correctAnswer: aircraft taking off or about to take off

children.text: Unless otherwise prescribed, what is the rule regarding level to be maintained by an aircraft flying IFR outside controlled airspace?

answers: 1 000 feet above the highest obstacle within 8 nautical miles of course
1 000 feet above the highest obstacle within 8 kilometres of the estimated position of the aircraft
2 000 feet above the highest obstacle within 8 nautical miles of course
2 000 feet above the highest obstacle within 8 kilometres of course
correctAnswer: 1 000 feet above the highest obstacle within 8 kilometres of the estimated position of the aircraft

children.text: Aircraft "A" with an ATC clearance is flying in VMC conditions within a control area. Aircraft "B" with no ATC clearance is approaching at approximately the same altitude and on a converging course. Which has the right of way?

answers: Aircraft "B" regardless of the direction "A" is approaching
Aircraft "A" regardless of the direction which "B" is approaching
Aircraft "A" if "B" is on its right
Aircraft "B" if "A" is on its left
correctAnswer: Aircraft "B" if "A" is on its left

children.text: Which of the following actions shall be taken in case of a controlled flight deviates from the track?

answers: Inform the ATC unit immediately
Notify ATC of the new track immediately and comply with instructions
Adjust the heading of aircraft to regain track as soon as practicable
If VMC, maintain this condition, waiting for the ATC instructions
correctAnswer: Adjust the heading of aircraft to regain track as soon as practicable

children.text: While on IFR flight, a pilot has an emergency which causes a deviation from an ATC clearance. What action must be taken?

answers: The appropriate ATC unit shall be notified of the action taken as soon as circumstances permit
Submit a detailed report to ATC within 24 hours
Squawk 7700
Request an amended clearance or cancel the IFR flight plan
correctAnswer: The appropriate ATC unit shall be notified of the action taken as soon as circumstances permit

children.text: A signalman will ask the pilot to apply parking brakes by the following signals:

answers: Crossing arms extended above his head
Horizontally moving his hands, fingers extended, palms toward ground
Arms down , palms facing inwards, moving arms from extended position inwards
Raise arm and hand, with fingers extended, horizontally in front of body , then clench fist
correctAnswer: Raise arm and hand, with fingers extended, horizontally in front of body , then clench fist

children.text: An aircraft is flying under Instrument Flight Rules in an area where the visibility is unlimited and the sky is clear (free of clouds), when it totally loses radio communications. The procedure to be followed is:

answers: land on the closest appropriate aerodrome, then advise Air Traffic Services of landing
continue flight onto destination, complying with last received clearances then with filed flight plan
descend to En-route Minimum Safe Altitude and join closest airfield open to IFR operations
adopt a VFR flight level and continue flight onto destination
correctAnswer: land on the closest appropriate aerodrome, then advise Air Traffic Services of landing

children.text: A red flare addressed to a flying aircraft means:

answers: Come back and land
Give way to another aircraft and hold the circuit
Dangerous airfield. Do not land
Not with standing any previous instructions, do not land for the time being
correctAnswer: Not with standing any previous instructions, do not land for the time being

children.text: A double white cross displayed horizontally in the signal area means:

answers: An area unit for the movement of aircraft
Need special precautions while approaching for landing
The aerodrome is being used by gliders and that glider flights are being performed
Special precautions must be observed due to bad state of the taxiways
correctAnswer: The aerodrome is being used by gliders and that glider flights are being performed

children.text: A signalman will ask the pilot to apply parking brakes by the following signals:

answers: Arms down, palms facing inwards, moving arms from extended position inwards
Horizontally moving hands, fingers extended, palms toward ground
Crossing arms extended above his head
Raising arm and hand horizontally in front of body, fingers extended then clenching fist
correctAnswer: Raising arm and hand horizontally in front of body, fingers extended then clenching fist

children.text: Aircraft wishing to conduct IFR flight within advisory airspace, but not electing to use the air traffic advisory service:

answers: need to file a flight plan
may file a flight plan under pilot's discretion
Shall nevertheless submit a flight plan but changes made thereto are not necessary to be notified
Shall nevertheless submit a flight plan and notify changes made thereto to the ATS unit providing that service
correctAnswer: Shall nevertheless submit a flight plan and notify changes made thereto to the ATS unit providing that service

children.text: An aircraft is considered to overtake another if it approaches the other aircraft from the rear on a line forming an angle of less than:

answers: 50 degrees with the plane of symmetry of the latter
70 degrees with the plane of symmetry of the latter
80 degrees with the plane of symmetry of the latter
60 degrees with the plane of symmetry of the latter
correctAnswer: 70 degrees with the plane of symmetry of the latter

children.text: Which provisions on a VFR-flight in Class E airspace are CORRECT?

answers: Service provided: Traffic Information as far as practical; ATC Clearance: not required
Service provided: Air Traffic Control Service; ATC Clearance: not required
Service provided: Air Traffic Control Service; ATC Clearance: required
Service provided: Traffic Information as far as practical; ATC Clearance: required
correctAnswer: Service provided: Traffic Information as far as practical; ATC Clearance: not required

children.text: VMC minima for VFR flights in Class B airspace, above 3050m (10000 ft) AMSL, are

answers: 5 km visibility, 1500 m horizontal and 1000 ft vertical distance from clouds
No minima, VFR flights are not permitted
8 km visibility, and clear of clouds
8 km visibility, 1500 m horizontal and 1000 ft vertical distance from clouds
correctAnswer: 8 km visibility, 1500 m horizontal and 1000 ft vertical distance from clouds

children.text: During an IFR flight in VMC in controlled airspace you experience a two-way radio communication failure. You will:

answers: Select A7600 and continue according currenct flight plan to destination
Land at the nearest suitable aerodrome and inform ATC
Land at the nearest suitable aerodrome maintaining VMC and inform ATC
Descend to the flight level submitted for that portion of flight
correctAnswer: Land at the nearest suitable aerodrome maintaining VMC and inform ATC

children.text: When a controlled flight inadvertently deviates from its current flightplan, ATC has to be informed in case:

answers: it is a deviation from the track
the estimated time is in error by more than 10 minutes
the TAS varies by plus or minus 5% of the TAS notified in the flightplan
of an emergency
correctAnswer: the TAS varies by plus or minus 5% of the TAS notified in the flightplan

children.text: Where State has not established minimum IFR altitudes, the minimum height of an aircraft above the highest obstacle over high terrain, or in mountainous areas shall be for an IFR flight:

answers: at least 2000 feet within 8 KM of the estimated position
at least 1000 feet within 5KM of the estimated position
at least 2000 feet within 5KM of the estimated position
at least 1000 feet within 8 KM of the estimated position
correctAnswer: at least 2000 feet within 8 KM of the estimated position

children.text: An aircraft shall display, if so equipped, an anti-collision light:

answers: outside the daylight-period in flight, but not on the ground when it is being towed
outside the daylight-period at engine-start. During the daylight-period this is not applicable
while taxiing, but not when it is being towed
on the ground when the engines are running
correctAnswer: on the ground when the engines are running

children.text: The white dumb-bell with black perpendicular bar indicates that:

answers: taxiing need not be confined to the taxiways
landing, take-off and taxiing is allowed on runway and/or taxiway only
this aerodrome is using parallel runways
gliderflying is performed outside the landing area
correctAnswer: taxiing need not be confined to the taxiways

children.text: Your aircraft is intercepted by a military aircraft. The signals given by this aircraft conflict with ATC instructions. You should:

answers: follow the instructions of the intercepting aircraft
request ATC for other instructions
follow ATC instructions
select code A7500 on your transponder
correctAnswer: follow the instructions of the intercepting aircraft

children.text: An aircraft manoeuvering in an airport's circuit receives a series of red flashes from the control tower. This signifies that the aircraft must:

answers: give way to another aircraft
not land because the airport is not available for landing
return to land and that clearance to land will be communicated in due course
not land for the moment regardless of previous instructions
correctAnswer: not land because the airport is not available for landing

children.text: Whilst flying in an aerodrome's traffic circuit, an aircraft receives a series of green flashes from the tower. The aircraft:

answers: must give way to another aircraft
is cleared to land
must come back to land and the landing clearance will be sent in due time
must land immediately and clear the landing area
correctAnswer: must come back to land and the landing clearance will be sent in due time

children.text: While taxying an aircraft receives the following light signal from the control tower: series of red flashes. This signal means that the aircraft

answers: may continue to taxi to the take-off area
must return to its point of departure
must stop
must vacate the landing area in use
correctAnswer: must vacate the landing area in use

children.text: While taxiing, an aircraft receives from the airport controller the following light signal: a series of green flashes. This signal means that the aircraft:

answers: is cleared for take-off
must stop
must return to its point of departure
may continue to taxy towards the take-off area
correctAnswer: may continue to taxy towards the take-off area

children.text: Within uncontrolled airspace, the first usable level in IFR must provide a 500 ft margin above the following two levels:

answers: FL 30 or 100 ft AGL
3 000 ft AMSL or 1 000ft AGL
FL 30 or 1 500 ft AGL
3 000 ft AMSL or 1 500 ft AGL
correctAnswer: 3 000 ft AMSL or 1 000ft AGL

children.text: (IR) Where no minimum flight altitudes have been established for IFR flights, which statement concerning minimum flight altitudes for IFR flights is correct:

answers: Over high terrain or in mountainous areas, at a level which is at least 300 m (1000 ft.) above the highest obstacle located within 8 km of the estimated position of the aircraft
Over high terrain or in mountainous areas, at a level which is at least 300 m (1000 ft.) above the highest obstacle located within 8 nm of the estimated position of the aircraft
Over high terrain or in mountainous areas, at a level which is at least 600 m (2000 ft.) above the highest obstacle located within 8 nm of the estimated position of the aircraft
Over high terrain or in mountainous areas, at a level which is at least 600 m (2000 ft.) above the highest obstacle located within 8 km of the estimated position of the aircraft
correctAnswer: Over high terrain or in mountainous areas, at a level which is at least 600 m (2000 ft.) above the highest obstacle located within 8 km of the estimated position of the aircraft

children.text: (IR) On an IFR flight, you experience a total communication failure in conditions of no clouds and in unlimited visibility. What should you do?

answers: Descend to en route minimum sector altitude and land at the nearest suitable IFR aerodrome
Continue under IFR and follow filed flight plan
Land at the nearest suitable aerodrome and inform ATS immediately
Continue to destination in VMc
correctAnswer: Land at the nearest suitable aerodrome and inform ATS immediately

children.text: (IR) An ETA for an IFR flight refers to the following:

answers: None of the above
Touch down
FAF/FAP
IAF
correctAnswer: IAF

children.text: What does the abbreviation OIS mean?

answers: Obstruction in surface
Obstacle identification slope
Obstacle in surface
Obstacle identification surface
correctAnswer: Obstacle identification surface

children.text: What does the abbreviation DER mean?

answers: Distance end of route
Depature end of runway
Departure end of route
Distance end of runway
correctAnswer: Depature end of runway

children.text: The MSA, which must be established around a navigation facility, is in general valid within a sector of:

answers: 30 NM
10 NM
25 NM
15 NM
correctAnswer: 25 NM

children.text: (IR) What does the abbreviation DER mean?

answers: Dead reckoning
Departure end of runway
Departure end routing
Distance error rectification
correctAnswer: Departure end of runway

children.text: As a rule, while establishing the departure procedures, the operator reckons that aeroplane has a climb gradient of:

answers: 2.4% with all engines operating and 1.5% with one engine inoperative
2.4% with two engines, 2.7% with three engines, 3% with four engines
3.3% with all engines operating
5% with all engines operating and a climb gradient margin respectively of 0.8%, 0.9%, 1% with two, three and four engines, taking in account one engine inoperative
correctAnswer: 2.4% with two engines, 2.7% with three engines, 3% with four engines

children.text: A four-engine aeroplane is about to take-off from an airport where poor weather conditions are prevailing. The closest accessible aerodrome is three flying hours away. The take-off minima to be observed at departure airfield are:

answers: VH (visibility horizontal) greater or equal to VH required for landing on the runway to be used
ceiling greater or equal to DH or MDH, and VH (horizontal visibility) greater or equal to VH required for landing, with an available instrument approach procedure to be envisaged with one engine out
ceiling greater or equal to DH/MDH, and VH (horizontal visibility) greater or equal to VH required for landing, with an available instrument approach procedure
VH (visibility horizontal) greater or equal to VH required for landing, and ceiling greater or equal to ceiling required for landing, with an available instrument approach procedure
correctAnswer: ceiling greater or equal to DH/MDH, and VH (horizontal visibility) greater or equal to VH required for landing, with an available instrument approach procedure

children.text: If in an instrument departure procedure the track to be followed by the aeroplane is published, the pilot is expected:

answers: To request from ATC different heading for wind correction
To ignore the wind and proceed on an heading equal to the track
To request clearance from ATC for applying a wind correction
To correct for known wind to remain within the protected airspace
correctAnswer: To correct for known wind to remain within the protected airspace

children.text: In general, which is the main factor that dictates the design of an instrument departure procedure?

answers: Navigation aids
ATC requirements
The terrain surrounding the airport
Airspace restrictions
correctAnswer: The terrain surrounding the airport

children.text: In an instrument departure procedure the minimum obstacle clearance at the departure end of runway equals:

answers: 3.3 % gradient
0.8 % gradient
0 ft
35 ft
correctAnswer: 0 ft

children.text: In a straight departure, the initial departure track is of the aligment of the runway centre line within:
Turning departures provide track guidance within:

answers: 10 Km
5 Km
20 Km
15 Km
correctAnswer: 10 Km

children.text: We can distinguish two types of departure routes. During a straight departure the initial departure track is within:

answers: 25° of the alignment of the runway centre-line
10° of the alignment of the runway centre-line
15° of the alignment of the runway centre-line
5° of the alignment of the runway centre-line
correctAnswer: 15° of the alignment of the runway centre-line

children.text: (IR) Who is responsible for the development of the contingency procedures required to cover the case of engine failure during an instrument departure, which occurs after V1?

answers: The operator of the aircraft
ATC will provide radar vector for obstacle clearance
The pilot in close co-operation with ATc
The designer of the procedure (normally the State)
correctAnswer: The operator of the aircraft

children.text: (IR) Which of the following standard instrument departures is not a straight departure? A) A departure where the initial departure track differs more than 20° from the runway alignment. B) A departure where the initial departure track differs more than 15° from the runway alignment. C) A departure where the initial departure track differs more than 10° from the runway alignment. D) A departure where the initial departure track differs more than 5° from the runway alignment.

answers: A
A and B
A, B and C
A, B, C and D
correctAnswer: A and B

children.text: During circling-to-land (with or without prescribed flight tracks), the maximum allowed airspeed  for a Cat B aeroplane, in order to remain within the protection envelope, is:

answers: 150 kt
120 kt
135 kt
125 kt
correctAnswer: 135 kt

children.text: The protection areas associated with instrument approach procedures are determined with the assumption that turns are performed at a bank angle of:

answers: 25° or the bank angle giving a 3°/s turn rate, whichever is lower, for departure and approach instrument procedures, 25° for circling-to-land with prescribed flight tracks and 15° for missed approach procedures

25° or the bank angle giving a 3°/s turn rate, whichever is lower, for departure, approach or missed approach instrument procedures, as well as circling-to-land (with or without prescribed flight tracks)
The bank angle giving a 3°/s turn rate for all procedures with airspeed limitation related to aeroplane categories
25° or the bank angle giving a 3°/s turn rate, whichever is lower, for departure and approach instrument procedures, as well as circle-to-land, and 15° for missed approach procedures
correctAnswer: 25° or the bank angle giving a 3°/s turn rate, whichever is lower, for departure, approach or missed approach instrument procedures, as well as circling-to-land (with or without prescribed flight tracks)

children.text: Under which conditions may an aircraft on a straight-in-VOR approach continue its descend below the OCA?

answers: When the aircraft has the control tower in sight
When the aircraft is in visual contact with the ground and with the runway lights in sight
When seems possible to land
When the aircraft is in contact with the ground but not with the runway in sight yet
correctAnswer: When the aircraft is in visual contact with the ground and with the runway lights in sight

children.text: A turn executed by the aircraft during the initial approach between the end of the outbound track and the beginning of the intermediate or final approach track is a:

answers: Reversal procedure
Base turn
Procedure turn
Race track
correctAnswer: Base turn

children.text: If a stepdown fix is established on the final approach track, a descend shall be made so as to:

answers: leave the intermediate approach altitude, step by step until reaching the MAPt
pass the fix at the rate of descent of 500 feet/min, which is obligatory
pass the fix not below the specified crossing altitude
follow approximately 50 feet above the nominal glide path
correctAnswer: pass the fix not below the specified crossing altitude

children.text: In the ILS-approach, the OCA is referenced to:

answers: Aeredrome reference point
Mean sea level
Relevant runway threshold
Aerodrome elevation
correctAnswer: Mean sea level
